name: create_event
description: Creates a new calendar event with specified attendees, time, location, and recurrence settings. Sends invitations to attendees and sets reminders as configured.

title.description: The display title or subject of the calendar event.
title.$comment: Kept as free-form string to support any event naming convention.

start_time.description: The start date and time of the event in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2024-09-15T09:00:00).
start_time.$comment: ISO 8601 chosen for unambiguous, timezone-portable datetime representation.

end_time.description: The end date and time of the event in ISO 8601 format. Must be after start_time.
end_time.$comment: End time is required to avoid ambiguous all-day vs timed event interpretation.

attendees.description: List of attendee email addresses to invite to the event.
attendees.$comment: Array of strings rather than objects to keep the interface simple; display names resolved server-side.

location.description: Physical address or virtual meeting URL for the event location.
location.$comment: Optional field; can be physical address or virtual link to support hybrid events.

description.description: Detailed agenda, notes, or body text for the calendar event invitation.
examples: Discuss Q3 OKRs and assign owners.
Annual performance review. Please bring self-assessment.
description.$comment: Optional free-text field; supports plain text. Rendering depends on calendar client.

timezone.description: IANA timezone identifier for interpreting start_time and end_time.
timezone.$comment: Enum restricted to major IANA timezones to prevent invalid inputs; covers ~95% of enterprise use cases.

recurrence.$comment: Simplified recurrence enum; complex RRULE patterns (e.g., every 2nd Tuesday) should use a dedicated recurrence rule field in advanced implementations.

reminder_minutes.description: Number of minutes before the event start to send a reminder notification.
reminder_minutes.$comment: Maximum set to 40320 (28 days) to prevent unreasonably far-ahead reminders. 0 means reminder at event start time.
